context: Evaluates whether the candidate correctly implements an AI-optimized documentation system with semantic indexing for an agentic pack. The system must produce three interconnected index files and documentation with proper YAML frontmatter to enable token-efficient document discovery.

checklist.name: Three required index files
checklist.description: The .ai-index/ directory contains all three required files: semantic-index.json, task-to-docs-mapping.json, and cross-reference-graph.json. All three files are valid JSON.

checklist.name: Semantic index entry fields
checklist.description: Each entry in semantic-index.json includes at minimum: id, title, path, category, semantic_keywords (array of discovery phrases), use_cases (array of task identifiers), and related_docs (array of related document IDs). The semantic_keywords are descriptive phrases suitable for AI keyword matching.

checklist.name: Task-to-docs mapping structure
checklist.description: task-to-docs-mapping.json maps common workflow task type keys (e.g., 'cve_remediation', 'vm_creation') to arrays of document paths that should be loaded for that workflow. At least two distinct task types are mapped.

checklist.name: Cross-reference graph structure
checklist.description: cross-reference-graph.json maps document IDs to objects containing a 'related' array of related document IDs, enabling agents to follow cross-reference chains.

checklist.name: Documentation YAML frontmatter
checklist.description: Documentation markdown files include YAML frontmatter with all mandatory fields: title, category, sources (array with title/url/date_accessed), tags, semantic_keywords, use_cases, related_docs, and last_updated. Sources reference official documentation URLs.

checklist.name: INDEX.md navigation structure
checklist.description: A docs/INDEX.md file exists as a navigation map that AI agents can read first to understand the documentation structure before querying the semantic index.
